steps.instruction: VY/2 media Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume Baker’s yeast 5 g/L 5 g CaCl 2 × 2H 2 O 1.36 g/L 1.36 g Vitamin B12 0.5 mg/L 0.5 mg Agar (Difco) 15 g/L 15 g Distilled water n/a 1,000 mL Note: Autoclave. Adjust to pH 7.2 with KOH.

steps.title: EDTA 0
steps.instruction: EDTA 0.5 M pH 7.5 Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume CaCl 2 0.18612 g/mL 46.53 g Distilled water n/a 250 mL Note: Filter sterilize the solution using a 0.22 μm sterile filter.

steps.title: Sorbitol 1 M Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: Sorbitol 1 M Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume Sorbitol 182 g/L 182 g Distilled water n/a 1,000 mL Note: Filter sterilize the solution using a 0.22 μm sterile filter. Storage: 6 months at room temperature (RT).

steps.title: SOS Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume Sorbitol 0
steps.instruction: SOS Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume Sorbitol 0.182 g/mL 9.1 g Yeast extract 0.0025 g/mL 0.125 g Bacto peptone 0.005 g/mL 0.25 g CaCl 2 6.5 mM 300 μL Distilled water n/a 50 mL Note: Filter sterilize the solution using a 0.2 μm sterile filter. Store for 6 months at RT. Aliquot in 5 mL volumes.

steps.title: Tris-HCl 1 M pH 7
steps.instruction: Tris-HCl 1 M pH 7.5 Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume Tris-HCl 1 M pH 7.5 121.4 g/L 121.4 g Distilled water n/a 1,000 mL Note: Filter sterilize the solution using a 0.2 μm sterile filter.

steps.title: SPEM pH 7

Store for 6 months at RT. Aliquot in 10 mL volumes.

steps.title: STC Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume Sorbitol 0
steps.instruction: STC Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume Sorbitol 0.182 g/mL 18.2 g Tris-HCl 1 M pH 7.5 0.01 M 1 mL CaCl 2 1 M 0.01 M 1 mL Distilled water n/a 100 mL Note: Filter sterilize the solution using a 0.2 μm sterile filter. Store for 6 months at RT. Aliquot in 1 mL volumes.

steps.title: For this protocol, we selected the myxobacterium A
steps.instruction: For this protocol, we selected the myxobacterium A. fasciculatus SBSr002 , which grows in VY/2 media with an incubation period of 8–14 days. Grow this myxobacterium in 10 mL of VY/2 media at 28 °C in a liquid culture (200–250 rpm, vented cap Greiner tubes). Note: Subculture (at least) every three weeks due to their slow growth. Change the liquid medium weekly with 10 mL of fresh medium. If there is no growth after 10 days, carefully split up the agar culture cubes, squeeze the material to the agar plate, and reincubate .

steps.title: Isolate genomic DNA from 10 mL...
steps.instruction: Isolate genomic DNA from 10 mL cultures of actively growing (exponential/mid-log) cultures of A. fasciculatus SBSr002, using the GenElute TM Bacterial Genomic DNA kit for Gram-negative, following the manufacturer’s instructions. Elute the DNA in Tris-EDTA solution. Prepare agarose plugs containing genomic DNA from this strain according to the protocol “Preparation of Agarose Embedded Bacterial DNA” by Bio-Rad (CHEF Genomic DNA Plug Kits) found at https://www.bio-rad.com/webroot/web/pdf/lsr/literature/LIT510.pdf. Prepare all plugs with 1% agarose and four different culture concentrations (1:1 with 60 μL of culture, 1:2 with 30 μL of culture, 1:4 with 15 μL of culture, and 1:8 with 7.5 μL of culture).

steps.title: Use Agentcourt AMPure XP beads to...
steps.instruction: Use Agentcourt AMPure XP beads to concentrate and purify 100 μL of genomic DNA down to 10 μL, with 180 μL of beads (1.8× proportion) following the manufacturer’s instructions. B. Rapid barcoding sequencing (SQK-RBK

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: Before your first run, we recommend all new users watch the “Rapid Barcoding Sequencing (SQK-RBK004)” barcoding image found at https://nanoporetech.com/document/rapid-barcoding-sequencing-sqk-rbk004 . All steps in this section are performed at room temperature (RT).

steps.title: Thaw Rapid Barcoding kit components at...
steps.instruction: Thaw Rapid Barcoding kit components at RT, spin down briefly using a microfuge, and mix by pipetting in the order indicated below: a. Fragmentation mix RB01-

steps.title: not frozen, briefly spin down, mix well by pipetting
steps.instruction: not frozen, briefly spin down, mix well by pipetting. b. Rapid adapter (RAP): not frozen, briefly spin down, mix well by pipetting. c. Sequencing buffer (SQB): thaw at RT, briefly spin down, mix well by pipetting*. d. Loading beads (LB): thaw at RT, briefly spin down, mix by pipetting or vortexing immediately before use. e. Flush buffer (FLB)-1 tube: thaw at RT, briefly spin down, mix well by pipetting*. f. Flush tether (FLT): thaw at RT, briefly spin down, mix well by pipetting. Note: Vortexing, followed by a brief spin in a microfuge, is recommended for SQB and FLB.

steps.title: Resuspend the DNA in nuclease-free water
steps.instruction: Resuspend the DNA in nuclease-free water.

steps.title: Transfer ~400 ng of genomic DNA...
steps.instruction: Transfer ~400 ng of genomic DNA into a DNA LoBind tube.

steps.title: Adjust the volume to 7
steps.instruction: Adjust the volume to 7.5 μL with nuclease-free water.

steps.title: Mix by finger flicking the tube to avoid unwanted shearing
steps.instruction: Mix by finger flicking the tube to avoid unwanted shearing.

steps.title: Spin down briefly in a microfuge
steps.instruction: Spin down briefly in a microfuge.

steps.title: In a 0.2 mL thin-walled PCR tube, mix the following
steps.instruction: In a 0.2 mL thin-walled PCR tube, mix the following: a. 7.5 μL of 400 ng of template DNA. b. 2.5 μL of fragmentation mix RB01-12 (one for each sample).

steps.title: Mix gently by finger flicking the tube and spin down
steps.instruction: Mix gently by finger flicking the tube and spin down.

steps.title: Incubate the tube at 30 °C...
steps.instruction: Incubate the tube at 30 °C for 1 min and then at 80 °C for 1 min. Briefly (2 min), put the tube on ice to cool it down.

steps.title: Pool the barcoded samples in a 1
steps.instruction: Pool the barcoded samples in a 1.5 mL Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube. The expected volume is ~10 μL per sample.

steps.title: Resuspend the AMPure XP beads by vortexing
steps.instruction: Resuspend the AMPure XP beads by vortexing.

steps.title: To the entire pooled barcoded sample...
steps.instruction: To the entire pooled barcoded sample from step B10, add an equal volume of resuspended AMPure XP beads and mix by flicking the tube.

steps.title: Incubate on a Hula mixer (rotator...
steps.instruction: Incubate on a Hula mixer (rotator mixer) for 5 min at RT.

steps.title: Prepare 500 μL of fresh 70% ethanol in nuclease-free water
steps.instruction: Prepare 500 μL of fresh 70% ethanol in nuclease-free water.

steps.title: Spin down the sample and pellet on a magnet
steps.instruction: Spin down the sample and pellet on a magnet. Keep the tube on the magnet and pipette off the supernatant.

steps.title: Keep the tube on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Keep the tube on the magnet and wash the beads with 200 μL of freshly prepared 70% ethanol without disturbing the pellet. Remove the ethanol using a pipette and discard.

steps.title: Repeat the previous step
steps.instruction: Repeat the previous step.

steps.title: Spin down and place the tube back on the magnet
steps.instruction: Spin down and place the tube back on the magnet. Pipette off any residual 70% ethanol. Allow to dry for 5 min at RT.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and resuspend the pellet in 10 μL of 10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 7.5–8.0, with 50 mM NaCl. Incubate for 2 min at RT.

steps.title: Pellet the beads on a magnet...
steps.instruction: Pellet the beads on a magnet until the eluate is clear and colorless, for at least 1 min.

steps.title: Remove and retain 10 μL of...
steps.instruction: Remove and retain 10 μL of eluate into a clean 1.5 mL Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube. Note: Dispose of the pelleted beads.

steps.title: Add 1 μL of RAP to...
steps.instruction: Add 1 μL of RAP to 10 μL of barcoded DNA.

steps.title: Mix gently by finger flicking the tube and spin down
steps.instruction: Mix gently by finger flicking the tube and spin down.

steps.title: Incubate the reaction for 5 min at RT. Note
steps.instruction: Incubate the reaction for 5 min at RT. Note: The prepared library is used for loading into the MinION flow cell. Store the library on ice until ready to load. C. Priming and loading the SpotON flow cell Notes:

steps.title: We recommend that all new users...
steps.instruction: We recommend that all new users watch the “Priming and loading your flow cell” video before their first run; this video can be found at https://community.nanoporetech.com/nanopore_learning/lessons/priming-and-loading-your-flow-cell .

steps.title: The sequencing tether (SQT) tube will...
steps.instruction: The sequencing tether (SQT) tube will NOT be used in this protocol. It is provided in the kit for potential future product compatibility.

steps.title: Thaw the SQB, LB, FLT and...
steps.instruction: Thaw the SQB, LB, FLT and one tube of FB at RT.

steps.title: Mix the SQB, FLT, and FB...
steps.instruction: Mix the SQB, FLT, and FB tubes by vortexing (each tube on its own) and spin down at RT.

steps.title: Open the MinION Mk1B lid and...
steps.instruction: Open the MinION Mk1B lid and slide the flow cell under the clip.

steps.title: Slide the priming port cover clockwise...
steps.instruction: Slide the priming port cover clockwise to open the priming port. Note: Take care when drawing back the buffer from the flow cell. Do not remove more than 20–30 μL and make sure that the array of pores is covered by buffer at all times. Introducing air bubbles into the array can irreversibly damage pores.

steps.title: After opening the priming port, check...
steps.instruction: After opening the priming port, check for a small air bubble under the cover. Draw back a small volume to remove any bubbles (a few microliters): a. Set a P1,000 pipette to 200 μL. b. Insert the tip into the priming port. c. Turn the wheel until the dial shows 220–230 μL or until you can see a small volume of buffer entering the pipette tip. Note: Visually check that there is a continuous buffer from the priming port across the sensor array. See a video of priming and loading your flow cells at https://community.nanoporetech.com/nanopore_learning/lessons/priming-and-loading-your-flow-cell .

steps.title: To prepare the flow cell priming...
steps.instruction: To prepare the flow cell priming mix, add 30 μL of thawed and mixed FLT directly to the tube of thawed and mixed FB and mix by vortexing at RT.

steps.title: Load 800 μL of the priming...
steps.instruction: Load 800 μL of the priming mix into the flow cell via the priming port, avoiding the introduction of air bubbles. Wait for 5 min. During this time, prepare the library for loading by following the steps below.

steps.title: Thoroughly mix the contents of the LB tubes by vortexing
steps.instruction: Thoroughly mix the contents of the LB tubes by vortexing.

steps.title: In a new tube, prepare the...
steps.instruction: In a new tube, prepare the library for loading as follows: a. 34 μL of SQB. b. 25.5 μL of LB, mixed immediately before use. c. 4.5 μL of nuclease-free water. d. 11 μL of DNA library. Note: The LB tube contains a suspension of beads. These beads settle very quickly. It is vital that they are mixed immediately before use.

steps.title: Complete the flow cell priming
steps.instruction: Complete the flow cell priming: a. Gently lift the SpotON sample port cover to make the SpotON sample port accessible. b. Load 200 μL of the priming mix into the flow cell via the priming port (not the SpotON sample port), avoiding the introduction of air bubbles.

steps.title: Mix the prepared library gently by...
steps.instruction: Mix the prepared library gently by pipetting up and down just prior to loading.

steps.title: Add 75 μL of sample to...
steps.instruction: Add 75 μL of sample to the flow cell via the SpotON sample port in a dropwise fashion. Ensure each drop flows into the port before adding the next.

steps.title: Gently replace the SpotON sample port...
steps.instruction: Gently replace the SpotON sample port cover, making sure the bung enters the SpotON port, and close the priming port. Note: Install the light shield on your flow cell as soon as the library has been loaded for optimal sequencing output. We recommend leaving the light shield on the flow cell when the library is loaded, including during any washing and reloading steps. The shield can be removed when the library has been removed from the flow cell.

steps.title: Place the light shield onto the...
steps.instruction: Place the light shield onto the flow cell by carefully placing the leading edge of the light shield against the clip. Notes:

steps.title: Do not force the light shield underneath the clip
steps.instruction: Do not force the light shield underneath the clip. Gently lower the light shield onto the flow cell. The light shield should sit around the SpotON cover, covering the entire top section of the flow cell.

steps.title: The MinION flow cell light shield...
steps.instruction: The MinION flow cell light shield is not secured to the flow cell; careful handling is required after installation.

steps.title: Close the device lid and set...
steps.instruction: Close the device lid and set up a sequencing run on MinKNOW.

steps.title: Run the sequencing experiment configured to...
steps.instruction: Run the sequencing experiment configured to store Fast5 raw data files by the ONT MinION during sequencing. D. Genome assembly

steps.title: Base-call, demultiplex, and trim the Fast5...
steps.instruction: Base-call, demultiplex, and trim the Fast5 raw data files using the data processing toolkit Guppy version 4.4.1 https://github.com/nanoporetech/pyguppyclient . Use the following commands: guppy_basecaller --input_path “input folder” --save_path “output folder” g --barcode_kits SQK-RBK004 --trim_barcodes

steps.title: Assemble the ONT data into contigs...
steps.instruction: Assemble the ONT data into contigs using Flye genome assembler v2.9.5 ( https://github.com/mikolmogorov/Flye ) [23]. Use the following commands: Flye --nano-raw “inputfile.fastq” --out-dir “output folder”

steps.title: Polish or correct base-calling errors in...
steps.instruction: Polish or correct base-calling errors in the sequences generated by ONT using Medaka polisher v1.12.0 ( https://github.com/nanoporetech/medaka ). Use the following commands: medaka_consensus -i “basecalled sample” -d “assembled fastq file” -o output folder

steps.title: Upload the draft genome assembly or contigs to antiSMASH 7
steps.instruction: Upload the draft genome assembly or contigs to antiSMASH 7.1 ( https://github.com/antismash/antismash/releases ) [8] with the default strictness (relaxed) of detection. A relaxed strictness means that the tool detects well-defined clusters containing all required parts and partial clusters missing one or more functional parts. This version of antiSMASH includes an RRE-containing detection rule that enables the identification of potentially novel RiPPs clusters. In addition, all extra features during the analysis are selected. The flowchart of the computational pipeline can be found in Figure 1 . Use the following commands: run_antismash input.fasta outputfolder --genefinding-tool prodigal --cb-general --cb-knownclusters --cb-subclusters --asf --pfam2go --smcog-trees Figure

steps.title: Pipeline from the raw data to...
steps.instruction: Pipeline from the raw data to identification of biosynthetic gene clusters (BGCs). Fast5 raw data files generated by the Oxford Nanopore Technology (ONT) MinION are base-called, demultiplexed, and trimmed using the data processing toolkit Guppy. Grep barcode is used to locate which reads contain a specific barcode. Flye genome assembler is used to assemble the ONT data into contigs. Medaka polisher is used to polish or correct base-calling errors in the sequences generated by ONT. The draft genome assembly or contigs are uploaded to antiSMASH.

steps.title: Assess the quality and completeness of...
steps.instruction: Assess the quality and completeness of the assembled genome using checkM v1.2.3 ( https://github.com/Ecogenomics/CheckM ) [24] and BUSCO v5.6.0 ( https://gitlab.com/ezlab/busco ) [25]. Note: CheckM commands used: checkm lineage_wf checkm qa checkm lineage BUSCO commands used: busco -i -o -l -m genome

steps.title: Upload individual BGCs to Pfam v33
steps.instruction: Upload individual BGCs to Pfam v33.1 [26] and BLAST v2.16.0 [27] to predict the function of the closest homolog based on similarity between sequences. BLAST was used in the blastn mode for query (nucleotide) and database (nucleotide) search. Note: Pfam was used under the default settings.

steps.title: Annotate the assembled genome using Bakta v1
steps.instruction: Annotate the assembled genome using Bakta v1.9.3 ( https://github.com/oschwengers/bakta ) [28]. Note: BAKTA commands used: bakta --db /path/to/bakta_db --output bakta_out genome.fasta

steps.title: Classify gene functions into categories using...
steps.instruction: Classify gene functions into categories using the COG classifier tool v1.0.5 ( https://github.com/moshi4/COGclassifier ) [29]. Note: COGclassifier commands used: COGclassifier -i -o E. PCR amplification, digestion, and ligation steps prior to TAR cloning

steps.title: Amplify the oriT element from the...
steps.instruction: Amplify the oriT element from the plasmid pk18mobsacB with primers 1–2 (Table S1). See thermocycling conditions in Table 1 .

steps.title: Digest the resulting amplicon and the...
steps.instruction: Digest the resulting amplicon and the plasmid pCC1FOS with CutSmart ® Buffer and the following restriction enzymes (New England Biolabs): HpaI and BamHI-HF. Follow the manufacturer’s instructions for digestion. Note: Use 1 µg of DNA (plasmid and insert), with an initial enzyme-to-DNA ratio of 5–10 units of enzyme per µg of DNA. The insert-to-plasmid ratio is typically 1:1 to 3:1 for ligation.

steps.title: Purify digested DNA
steps.instruction: Purify digested DNA. Follow the manufacturer’s instructions of the NucleoSpin Gel and PCR Clean-up kit for purification.

steps.title: Ligate pCC1FOS with oriT to generate...
steps.instruction: Ligate pCC1FOS with oriT to generate pCC1FOS-oriT with T4 DNA ligase and T4 DNA ligase reaction buffer for 1 h at RT. Follow the manufacturer’s instructions for ligation. Note: The insert-to-plasmid ratio is typically 1:1 to 3:1 for ligation.

steps.title: Transform each ligation (with a total...
steps.instruction: Transform each ligation (with a total volume of 10 μL) into E. coli DH5α competent cells using the following steps: a. Thaw the cells on ice. b. Quickly add 10 μL of the ligation to the competent cells, next to the flame, mix gently, and incubate on ice for 30 min. c. Heat-shock the cells for 90 s at 42 °C in a preheated water bath and place the cells back on ice for 5 min. d. Add 1 mL of prewarmed LB medium to each tube and incubate at 37 °C for 60 min at 750 rpm. e. Plate 100 μL of the transformed bacteria on a 20 mL LB agar plate supplemented with chloramphenicol (34 μg/mL) and spread the resuspension throughout the plate. f. Centrifuge at 7,200× g for 1 min at RT and remove most of the medium with a pipette, leaving 100 μL to resuspend the rest. g. Pipette the resuspension onto a 20 mL LB agar plate supplemented with chloramphenicol (34 μg/mL) and spread the resuspension throughout the plate. h. Wait until the plate has dried, next to the flame. i. Incubate plates overnight at 37 °C. j. Isolate colonies in chloramphenicol (34 μg/mL) and grow them overnight at 37 °C and 250 rpm on 10 mL of LB media with chloramphenicol (34 μg/mL) for plasmid extraction. k. Isolate plasmid using GeneJET Plasmid Miniprep kit.

steps.title: Linearize 1 µg of pCC1FOS-oriT by...
steps.instruction: Linearize 1 µg of pCC1FOS-oriT by PCR amplification using primers 2 and 3 (Table S1). See thermocycling conditions in Table 1 . Table

steps.title: Thermocycling conditions for Q5 ® high-fidelity...
steps.instruction: Thermocycling conditions for Q5 ® high-fidelity DNA polymerase Cycle step Temperature Time Cycles Initial denaturation 98 °C 10 min 1 Denaturation 98 °C 20 s 30 Annealing 50–72 °C (adjusted according to the melting temperature of primers) 20 s Extension 72 °C 20–30 s/kb Final extension 72 °C 2–10 min (adjusted to size of amplicon) 1 Hold 10 °C ∞ -

steps.title: Amplify the CEN/ARS sequence from the plasmid pRS314 (Table S
steps.instruction: Amplify the CEN/ARS sequence from the plasmid pRS314 (Table S

steps.title: using primers 4–5 (Table S1)
steps.instruction: using primers 4–5 (Table S1). This genetic element is necessary to maintain the plasmid in yeast cells. See thermocycling conditions in Table 1 .

steps.title: Amplify the gentamicin resistance gene (...
steps.instruction: Amplify the gentamicin resistance gene ( GmR ) from a pSEVA62 backbone using primers 6–7 (Table S1). See thermocycling conditions in Table 1 .

steps.title: Incorporate the Lox66 site through PCR...
steps.instruction: Incorporate the Lox66 site through PCR amplification during an overlap PCR fusing CEN/ARS elements and the GmR gene using primers 4 and 7 (Table S1). See thermocycling conditions in Table 1 .

steps.title: Incorporate the CEN/ARS-GmR-lox66 in the vector...
steps.instruction: Incorporate the CEN/ARS-GmR-lox66 in the vector pSB1C3 with Golden Gate assembly. a. Dilute the target DNA fragment according to its size and concentration. b. Add 2 μL of assembly mix, 1 μL of linearized vector, 1 μL of diluted DNA fragment (insert), and water up to 8 μL. c. Set thermocycling conditions according to Table 2 .

steps.title: Linearize 300 ng of CEN/ARS-GmR-lox66 by...
steps.instruction: Linearize 300 ng of CEN/ARS-GmR-lox66 by PCR amplification for TAR cloning using primers 4–7 (Table S1). See thermocycling conditions in Table 1 .

steps.title: Incorporate the Loxm2/71 by amplification of...
steps.instruction: Incorporate the Loxm2/71 by amplification of the LEU2 using primers 8–9 (Table S

steps.title: from the plasmid pRS315 (Table S2)
steps.instruction: from the plasmid pRS315 (Table S2). See thermocycling conditions in Table 1 .

steps.title: Amplify the Cre recombinase from the iGEM Part:BBa_K112122 ( http://parts
steps.instruction: Amplify the Cre recombinase from the iGEM Part:BBa_K112122 ( http://parts.igem.org/Main_Page ) using primers 10–11 (Table S1). See thermocycling conditions in Table 1 .

steps.title: Assemble CRE-LEU2-loxm2/71 by overlap PCR using primers 12–15 (Table S1)
steps.instruction: Assemble CRE-LEU2-loxm2/71 by overlap PCR using primers 12–15 (Table S1). See thermocycling conditions in Table 1 .

steps.title: Incorporate CRE-LEU2-lox2/71 in the vector pSB1C3...
steps.instruction: Incorporate CRE-LEU2-lox2/71 in the vector pSB1C3 with Golden Gate assembly using primers 8–11 (to linearize vector). See thermocycling conditions in Table 1 . a. Dilute the target DNA fragment according to its size and concentration. b. Add 2 μL of assembly mix, 1 μL of linearized vector, 1 μL of diluted DNA fragment (insert), and water up to 8 μL. c. Set thermocycling conditions according to Table 2 . Table

steps.title: Thermocycling conditions for Golden Gate assembly...
steps.instruction: Thermocycling conditions for Golden Gate assembly Temperature Time Cycles 37 °C 20 min 1 16 °C 4 min 30 37 °C 3 min 50 °C 10 min 1 80 °C 10 min 1

steps.title: Linearize 300 ng of CRE-LEU2-lox2/71 by...
steps.instruction: Linearize 300 ng of CRE-LEU2-lox2/71 by PCR amplification for TAR cloning using primers 12 and

steps.title: See thermocycling conditions in Table 1
steps.instruction: See thermocycling conditions in Table 1 .

steps.title: Linearize 300 ng of each of...
steps.instruction: Linearize 300 ng of each of the five fragments forming the lanthipeptide BGC by PCR amplification using primers 16–25 (Table S1). See thermocycling conditions in Table 1 . Note: Design each cassette, amplicon, and linearized plasmid to contain 60-bp overhangs with the previous and following DNA fragment, enabling the capture of the lanthipeptide BGC, CEN/ARS-GmR-lox66, CRE-LEU2-lox2/71, and pCC1FOS-oriT in one step using transformation-associated recombination (TAR) cloning in yeast ( Figure 2 ). Figure

steps.title: TAR cloning in yeast using 5...
steps.instruction: TAR cloning in yeast using 5 kb BGC amplicons, CEN/ARS-GmR-lox66, pCC1FOS-oriT, and CRE-LEU2-loxm2/71. (A) PCR amplification, digestion, and ligation of PCC1FOS and oriT. (B) Transformation of assembled PCC1FOS-oriT and 5 kb BGC amplicons in yeast. (C) BGC capture in yeast. F. Transformation in yeast

steps.title: Start yeast pre-culture
steps.instruction: Start yeast pre-culture. Start yeast cultures (50 μL from glycerol stock) in 5 mL of the appropriate medium (e.g., YPDA, min SD-LEU).

steps.title: Incubate overnight at 30 °C and...
steps.instruction: Incubate overnight at 30 °C and 190 rpm (starting around 6 pm). Autoclave enough 500 mL Erlenmeyer flasks for day

steps.title: Measure the OD 600 nm (dilution 1/10)
steps.instruction: Measure the OD 600 nm (dilution 1/10). Measure the OD 600 nm in the morning (~9:30 am) and dilute accordingly in 5 mL of the appropriate medium, aiming at OD 600 nm = 2 by 6 pm. Check OD 600 nm again at ~2 pm and 6 pm.

steps.title: Dilute accordingly, aiming at OD 600...
steps.instruction: Dilute accordingly, aiming at OD 600 nm = 2 the next day at 4 pm, keeping in mind a generation time of 90 min. Note: The calculation starts with an OD 600 nm =2 at 4 pm and then OD 600 nm =1 at 2:30 pm. Following this generation time, an OD 600 nm =0.000061035 is reached at 5:30 pm. Calculate the dilution using the following formula: C 1 v 1 = C 2 v 2 Where C 1 is the measured OD 600 nm on day 2, v 1 is the initial volume to be calculated for the dilution, C 2 is the final OD 600 nm = 0.000061035, and v 2 is the final volume of 100 mL. Yeast culture

steps.title: v 1 ( 0
steps.instruction: v 1 ( 0.000061035 ) ( 100 m l ) C 1 Note: Adjust C 2 according to the calculations with the generation time.

steps.title: Inoculate 2 × 100 mL of...
steps.instruction: Inoculate 2 × 100 mL of yeast culture with appropriate medium (use 500 mL Erlenmeyer flask) with the calculated volume in microliters.

steps.title: Incubate at 30 °C at 190...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 30 °C at 190 rpm (until around 4 pm).

steps.title: Measure the OD 600 nm (dilution 1/10). Note
steps.instruction: Measure the OD 600 nm (dilution 1/10). Note: The expected OD 600 nm is between 2.6 and 2.

steps.title: Collect the cells by centrifugation at...
steps.instruction: Collect the cells by centrifugation at 1,892× g for 3 min at RT.

steps.title: Resuspend each culture in 20 mL of sorbitol 1 M
steps.instruction: Resuspend each culture in 20 mL of sorbitol 1 M.

steps.title: Divide each culture into 2× 50 mL tubes to centrifuge
steps.instruction: Divide each culture into 2× 50 mL tubes to centrifuge.

steps.title: Resuspend pellet in 20 mL of sorbitol
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet in 20 mL of sorbitol.

steps.title: Transfer to the other tube to resuspend the other pellet
steps.instruction: Transfer to the other tube to resuspend the other pellet. Store at 4 °C overnight.

steps.title: Yeast transformation. Before starting, prepare the following
steps.instruction: Yeast transformation. Before starting, prepare the following: a. Prepare the PEG 20% and top agar (top agar can be prepared in advance and kept at 65 °C). b. Refrigerate the centrifuge at 4 °C 15 min before centrifugation. c. Put the sorbitol plates at 30 °C. d. Prepare ~10 Spectro cuvettes with 1.35 mL of sorbitol 1 M and ~10 with 1.35 mL of SDS 2%. e. Prepare two 50 mL Falcons, each with 40 mL of sorbitol 1 M. f. Switch on the OD meter at 600 nm and blank with sorbitol 1 M.

steps.title: Collect the yeast cells by centrifugation...
steps.instruction: Collect the yeast cells by centrifugation at 1,892× g for 3 min at RT.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 10 mL of SPEM
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 10 mL of SPEM.

steps.title: Add 20 μL of β-Mercaptoethanol and...
steps.instruction: Add 20 μL of β-Mercaptoethanol and 20 μL of Zymolyase-100T into the pellet from step F15 inside the fume hood. Keep the tube with sorbitol close.

steps.title: Incubate in your hands for 5 min with mild agitation
steps.instruction: Incubate in your hands for 5 min with mild agitation. CRITICAL STEP: Overincubation can lead to a reduced number of correct transformants or no transformants at all due to excessive spheroplast lysis.

steps.title: Verify the OD 600 nm of...
steps.instruction: Verify the OD 600 nm of the following two sample preparations (A and B):

steps.title: Sample A
steps.instruction: Sample A: With 150 μL of culture + 1.35 mL of sorbitol 1 M (1/10).

steps.title: Sample B
steps.instruction: Sample B: With 150 μL of culture + 1.35 mL of SDS 2% (1/10). Note: The ratio of OD 600 nm between A and B should be 3–4 (normally around A = 0.8 and B = 0.2). Measure at 5, 7, 9, and 11 min and so on (every 2 min). Take samples as indicated until reaching the desired values. CRITICAL STEP : Ratios beyond 4 can lead to a reduced number of correct transformants or no transformants at all due to excessive yeast cell damage.

steps.title: Quickly add 40 mL of sorbitol...
steps.instruction: Quickly add 40 mL of sorbitol 1 M, mix by inversion, and recover the cells by centrifugation at 1,314× g for 5 min at 4 °C. CRITICAL STEP : Not adding the sorbitol on time can lead to a reduced number of transformants or no transformants at all due to excessive spheroplast lysis.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and resuspend the pellet carefully in 20 mL of sorbitol 1 M using a 25 mL pipette.

steps.title: Add 30 mL of sorbitol 1...
steps.instruction: Add 30 mL of sorbitol 1 M, mix by inversion, and centrifuge at 1,314× g for 5 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant and remove the...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and remove the last drops with a pipette.

steps.title: Resuspend carefully in 1 mL of STC
steps.instruction: Resuspend carefully in 1 mL of STC: mix carefully by rotating the tube. Keep for 10 min at RT. Meanwhile, aliquot the DNA (use pipette tips with their ends cut off when transferring gDNA, since regular pipette tips can shear it mechanically when aspirating). You may keep the spheroplast for 1 h at RT at this point.

steps.title: Aliquot the following four linearized DNA fragments into a 1
steps.instruction: Aliquot the following four linearized DNA fragments into a 1.5 mL tube: a. 1 μg of PCR-linearized pCC1FOS-oriT. b. 300 ng of the linearized CEN/ARS-GmR-lox66 cassette. c. 300 ng of linearized CRE-LEU2-loxm2/71 cassette. d. 300 ng of each lanthipeptide BGC amplicon.

steps.title: Add 100 μL of spheroplasts to...
steps.instruction: Add 100 μL of spheroplasts to each tube, mix by gently finger flicking the tube, and incubate for 10 min at RT.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.5 mL of PEG 20%, mix by inversion (4× strongly), and incubate for 20 min at RT.

steps.title: Centrifuge the spheroplasts at 4,643× g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the spheroplasts at 4,643× g for 5 min at RT.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellets with 700 μL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellets with 700 μL of SOS (mix gently with the pipette).

steps.title: Incubate for 2 h at 30 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate for 2 h at 30 °C. Put the TOP agar in the water bath at 55 °C.

steps.title: Put 700 μL of spheroplasts of...
steps.instruction: Put 700 μL of spheroplasts of the transformation in a 15 mL tube and add 12 mL of TOP agar-leu agar.

steps.title: Mix immediately by inversion (4–5 times gently) and plate
steps.instruction: Mix immediately by inversion (4–5 times gently) and plate. Put back the TOP agar-leu at 55 °C. Wait until the plates are solid and incubate at 30 °C.

steps.title: The transformant colonies are normally seen...
steps.instruction: The transformant colonies are normally seen after 3 days, but they can be kept up to 5 days at 30 °C to facilitate picking the colonies. Note: Yeast colonies will grow on top of or within the TOP agar-leu overlay.

steps.title: Patch yeast transformants onto SD-leu plates
steps.instruction: Patch yeast transformants onto SD-leu plates. Incubate the plates at 30 °C for 1–2 days. G. Screening yeast transformants by colony PCR

steps.title: Prepare Phire TM Hot Start II...
steps.instruction: Prepare Phire TM Hot Start II DNA polymerase master mixes with the pairs of primers in Table 3 . The sequence of primers can be found in Table S

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: Given that colony PCR amplicons are expected to have different sizes, a multiplex PCR using multiple pairs of primers can be performed in one reaction. Table

steps.title: Colony PCR primers and expected amplicon...
steps.instruction: Colony PCR primers and expected amplicon sizes of the lanthipeptide BGC assembly Colony PCR amplicon Expected size (kb) Primers 1 0.6 26–27 2 0.7 28–29 3 1.5 32–33 4 1.7 34–35

steps.title: Prepare individual colony PCR reactions of...
steps.instruction: Prepare individual colony PCR reactions of 20 μL in PCR tubes.

steps.title: Pick S
steps.instruction: Pick S. cerevisiae W303 transformants with sterile tips and transfer them to PCR tubes with Phire master mix. Place each tip in a different PCR tube. Note: Barely touch yeast colonies, since taking excessive DNA template can inhibit the reaction or lead to nonspecific amplification.

steps.title: Discard tips after direct contact of...
steps.instruction: Discard tips after direct contact of the colony with each PCR reaction tube.

steps.title: Run the PCR according to the...
steps.instruction: Run the PCR according to the thermocycler conditions in Table 4 . Table

steps.title: Thermocycling conditions for colony PCR using...
steps.instruction: Thermocycling conditions for colony PCR using Phire TM Hot Start II DNA polymerase Cycle step Temperature Time Cycles Initial denaturation 98 °C 10 min 1 Denaturation 98 °C 15 s 20 Annealing 50–72 °C (adjusted according to the melting temperature of primers) 15 s Extension 72°C 10 s/kb Final extension 72°C 1 min 1 Hold 10 °C ∞ 1

steps.title: Prepare 100 mL of 1% agarose...
steps.instruction: Prepare 100 mL of 1% agarose gel in 1× TAE buffer with 10 μL of MIDORI Green Easy in a wide mini-sub cell casting tray with 2 × 26-well combs.

steps.title: Place the agarose gel in the...
steps.instruction: Place the agarose gel in the wide mini-sub cell apparatus and add enough 1× TAE to cover the gel. Load the first well of each comb with 4 μL of 1 kb Plus DNA ladder.

steps.title: Add 4 μL of 6× DNA...
steps.instruction: Add 4 μL of 6× DNA loading dye to each PCR reaction.

steps.title: Add 10 μL of each PCR...
steps.instruction: Add 10 μL of each PCR reaction starting from well number

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: Loading gels with 26-well combs can be facilitated using multi-channel pipettes.

steps.title: Run the gel at 100 V...
steps.instruction: Run the gel at 100 V (constant) for 30 min using a PowerPac Basic Power Supply (Mupid-One Electrophoresis Unit).

steps.title: Visualize the gel in a gel doc XR
steps.instruction: Visualize the gel in a gel doc XR. Follow the manufacturer’s instructions for visualization.

steps.title: Identify positive transformants with amplicons of...
steps.instruction: Identify positive transformants with amplicons of sizes shown in Table 3 and Figure 3 .

steps.title: Grow positive colonies of S
steps.instruction: Grow positive colonies of S. cerevisiae W303 overnight at 30 °C and 250 rpm in 10 mL of SD minimal media depleted of leucine. This is the same media as in sorb plates, but without agar. Figure

steps.title: Gel electrophoresis with amplicons 1, 2,...
steps.instruction: Gel electrophoresis with amplicons 1, 2, 3, and 4, as shown in Table 3 H. Isolation of total yeast DNA and transformation into TransforMax TM EPI300 TM E. coli cells

steps.title: Extract total DNA using the Yeast DNA Extraction kit
steps.instruction: Extract total DNA using the Yeast DNA Extraction kit. Follow the manufacturer’s instructions.

steps.title: Pipette the chilled DNA/bacteria solution into the chilled 0
steps.instruction: Pipette the chilled DNA/bacteria solution into the chilled 0.2 cm gap-width cuvette. Follow the manufacturer’s instructions.

steps.title: Electroporate E
steps.instruction: Electroporate E. coli cells in a gene pulser electroporation system with the following settings: 2.5 kV, 25 μF, 200 Ω.

steps.title: Immediately following the electroporation, add 1...
steps.instruction: Immediately following the electroporation, add 1 mL of LB liquid media for recovery.

steps.title: Transfer the electroporated bacteria in LB into a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the electroporated bacteria in LB into a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Incubate the 1
steps.instruction: Incubate the 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube at 37 °C for 1 h with shaking at 250 rpm.

steps.title: Plate 100 μL of the electroporated...
steps.instruction: Plate 100 μL of the electroporated bacteria on an LB agar plate containing 15 μg/mL gentamycin.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 7,200× g for 1...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 7,200× g for 1 min at RT, remove most of the medium with a pipette, resuspend the rest, and spread the resuspension throughout the plate containing 15 μg/mL gentamycin.

steps.title: Incubate the plate at 37 °C for 1–2 days
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate at 37 °C for 1–2 days.

steps.title: Grow positive colonies of E
steps.instruction: Grow positive colonies of E. coli cells overnight at 37 °C and 250 rpm in 10 mL of LB media with gentamycin (15 μg/mL).

steps.title: Isolate plasmid using GeneJET Plasmid Miniprep kit
steps.instruction: Isolate plasmid using GeneJET Plasmid Miniprep kit. Follow the manufacturer’s instructions for plasmid isolation.

steps.title: Verify the sequence of isolated plasmids from E
steps.instruction: Verify the sequence of isolated plasmids from E. coli EPI300 using whole plasmid sequencing (Plasmidsaurus inc.).

description: Validation of protocol This protocol has been used and validated in the following research article: Campos-Magaña et al. [18]. Enabling Access to Novel Bacterial Biosynthetic Potential From ONT Draft Genomic Data. Microbial Biotechnology. Figure 3 and Supplementary Figure 6. After extracting the shuttle vector from S. cerevisiae W303 and amplifying it in E. coli EPI300 cells, we demonstrated that the complete BGC was assembled as intended by whole plasmid sequencing using ONT (Plasmidsaurus inc.). Furthermore, we performed Sanger sequencing of the entire gene cluster assembled in the vector. The alignment of Sanger sequencing with the original ONT sequencing showed 26 mismatches and 93 gaps/insertions from a total of 26 kb (99.5% identity) as can be observed in Figure S6 in [18], demonstrating that ONT sequencing has achieved high levels of accuracy. To ensure that these mismatches are not mutations generated during the PCR amplification and assembly process, we also performed Sanger sequencing on the genomic DNA, and only two mismatches were found, all producing silent mutations. In all, the sequencing precision given by ONT allowed obtaining sufficient data to accurately assemble the whole gene cluster in a plasmid vector. The Sanger polished sequence of the BGC is available at ENA accession PRJEB72099.
